why_wrong: Logic Assign And Or: forces the main output to zero, so coarse compilation passes but behavioral stimulus points fail.
Logic Assign And Or: uses a shifted or inverted decision condition that can pass one mode but fails boundary cases.
Logic Assign And Or: mishandles reset or initialization, passing steady-state samples but failing startup or recovery checks.
Logic Assign And Or: updates on the wrong edge or event, so simple static samples pass but timed checks fail.
Logic Assign And Or: uses a wrong gain or scale factor, leaving shape plausible while calibrated checks fail.
